feed.title: Euronews
feed.subtitle: Combined Euronews feed: News, My Europe, No Comment, Culture, Sport, Travel, and Next.

entry.title: European rescue teams arrive in Venezuela in desperate bid to find quake survivors
entry.content: Tens of thousands of people remain unaccounted for and time is running out to find survivors following the quakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5, the strongest to hit the South American nation in more than a century.

entry.title: What are the most liveable European cities in the world?
entry.content: Urban populations continue to surge worldwide, bringing new opportunities and challenges. So which European cities stand out for quality of life in 2026?

entry.title: Watch: Why Serbia’s most powerful politican just quit the presidency
entry.content: Last weekend, President Aleksandar Vučić stunned the nation by announcing he will resign within weeks and call early presidential and parliamentary elections. However, do not mistake this for a total surrender.

entry.title: 'C'mon queen!': Naomi Osaka wows Wimbledon crowd with ‘Kill Bill’ inspired kimono
entry.content: Japan's four-time major winner paid tribute to her nation's culture by arriving for her opening match at Wimbledon in a full-length kimono - entirely white to adhere to the tournament's strict dress code rules.

entry.title: Rare Strawberry Moon shines over Lisbon
entry.content: The strawberry moon rises over Lisbon, drawing crowds to viewpoints across the city as locals and tourists watch the rare moon above the River Tagus.

entry.title: From lab to sky: Portuguese graphene that hides planes and drones could transform defence
entry.content: Portugal may be taking a key step in the race for stealth technology with a graphene-based radar-absorbing material that could make drones and warplanes almost undetectable.

entry.title: Newsletter: EU-Turkey relations in spotlight ahead of NATO summit
entry.content: Also in this newsletter: Brussels sets autumn deadline for progress on rebalancing China trade; and European Investment Bank (EIB) President Nadia Calviño speaks to Europe Today about Europe's competitiveness.

entry.title: Brazil fans erupt after dramatic World Cup win over Japan
entry.content: Brazil fans celebrate a dramatic 2-1 win over Japan in São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro and Houston after Gabriel Martinelli's late World Cup winner.

entry.title: €3bn Airbus loan shows Europe reclaiming strategic autonomy, EIB boss tells Euronews
entry.content: European Investment Bank President Calviño has told Euronews that her institution's €3 billion commitment to Airbus shows that Europe is "moving in the right direction" in an increasingly competitive world.

entry.title: On NATO's eastern flank: How Lithuania is strengthening its defences
entry.content: Military exercises, a historic German deployment and sitting in Moscow's shadow: Lithuania's Chief of the Defence Staff tells Euronews why the country believes deterrence is its strongest defence.

entry.title: 'Not very Christian': How Danish Gen Z are rethinking confirmation
entry.content: Young Danes are increasingly questioning the religious meaning of confirmation, a Christian rite that has become one of the country’s defining coming-of-age rituals, while turning to secular alternatives that keep the milestone without the church.

entry.title: Serbia heads for elections as EU-China trade tensions grow
entry.content: US and Iranian delegations return to Qatar for fresh talks as Brussels and Beijing work to resolve key trade disputes. Meanwhile, Serbia prepares for early elections after President Aleksandar Vučić announced his resignation.

entry.title: Paraguay fans celebrate historic win over Germany
entry.content: Fans in Asuncion celebrate as Paraguay beat Germany 4-3 on penalties after a 1-1 draw, securing one of the biggest upsets of the 2026 World Cup.

entry.title: Monaco parcel bomb blast wounds Ukrainian tycoon, suspected attacker flees to France
entry.content: A couple and a teenager were wounded in the explosion that struck around 9:00 pm local time in a residential building on a street along the border with France.

entry.title: From Sanremo to Cinque Terre: Italian Riviera’s best places for a luxury coastal escape
entry.content: As interest in the Italian Riviera soars, travel experts reveal the most luxurious coastal towns to visit in Liguria.

entry.title: Iran prepares largest state funeral in history for slain Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
entry.content: After months-long delays, Tehran is in the midst of organising a six-day, multi-city funeral for late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, amid tight security and fragile US-Iran talks over Hormuz, nuclear and sanctions issues.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 30th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 30th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Germany and Netherlands dumped out on penalties as Brazil seal late win
entry.content: Three World Cup knockout ties delivered drama on Monday night, with Paraguay and Morocco pulling off surprise penalty wins over Germany and the Netherlands, and Brazil beating Japan with a late winner.

entry.title: 2,500-year-old bronze chariot sheds light on Tartessos history
entry.content: CSIC archaeologists have unearthed in Guareña (Badajoz) a bronze chariot, Greek pottery, eastern ivories and an Egyptian alabaster vessel, evidence of a Tartessian trade network 2,500 years ago.

entry.title: Venezuela twin earthquakes: death toll rises above 1,700
entry.content: Venezuelan authorities now put the death toll from the twin 24 June quakes at more than 1,700, with 5,034 injured and up to 68,000 missing as UN-backed rescue efforts continue in La Guaira.

entry.title: Iran says delegation to visit Qatar this week but insists no negotiations scheduled with the US
entry.content: Tehran confirmed a delegation would travel to Doha to discuss the implementation of a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) amid conflicting signals from Washington over the nature and timing of the talks.

entry.title: EU will 'come to its senses' on Israel, former Netanyahu adviser says
entry.content: A former Israeli national security adviser told Euronews that the EU, which he called "irrelevant", will come to its senses and "realise who are the good guys, and who are the bad."

entry.title: Swiss yodellers rehearse in public fountains as Basel swelters
entry.content: Basel’s national yodelling festival unfolds during a heatwave as performers sing in fountains, compete across the city and close with street parades.

entry.title: EU lawmakers say they were blocked from fully inspecting Italy's migrant detention centre in Albania
entry.content: MEPs from the Greens/EFA group say staff refused to answer questions, blocked access to cells and provided no data during a visit to the Gjadër facility.

entry.title: Thousands take part in New York Pride parade
entry.content: Thousands join New York Pride parade in Manhattan with floats, music and political leaders, marking celebration and remembrance of LGBTQ+ rights.

entry.title: Eleven killed in France as parachute training aircraft crashes near Nancy
entry.content: A Pilatus PC-6 carrying five instructors, five student nurses, and a pilot went down 300 metres from the runway shortly after take-off.

entry.title: EU sets October deadline to get 'tangible' results with China
entry.content: As EU Trade Commissioner Maroš Šefčovič met his Chinese counterpart Wang Wentao in Brussels on Monday, he said he would travel to China this autumn. Germany’s trade Minister Katherina Reiche also met Wentao, with her ministry calling on Beijing to ensure “a level playing field".

entry.title: Pakistan airstrikes leave homes in ruins in Afghanistan
entry.content: Villagers inspect destroyed homes in Afghanistan's Paktia province after overnight airstrikes, as Afghan and Pakistani officials give differing accounts.

entry.title: China understands food security — the EU does not
entry.content: Europe thinks that it is self-sufficient in food, but the reality is that its supply chain depends on imports for meat production. The EU is structurally dependent on imported high-protein feed to raise farmed animals, particularly soy and maize, writes Nico Muzi in an OpEd for Euronews.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 29th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 29th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Thousands of migrants queue for repatriation in South African camps
entry.content: Over 15,000 Malawians are being repatriated from crowded Durban camps as South Africa enforces migration rules and regional governments organise returns.

entry.title: Explainer: Why Péter Magyar is reluctant to align with the EU on Ukraine
entry.content: Hungary's new prime minister ended Budapest's veto on Ukraine's EU accession talks in June, but is resisting further progress, citing minority rights, Western Balkans fairness, and domestic political pressures. Here's why he's digging in.

entry.title: McKinsey's Kelsey Robinson on why AI is creating more anxiety than impact in marketing
entry.content: At Cannes Lions, management consultant Kelsey Robinson breaks down McKinsey's new research showing a paradox in AI adoption: marketers are widely using AI and excited by it, but many also fear its impact on their roles.

entry.title: Neither pro nor con: EU declines to take stand on AC debate amid brutal heatwave
entry.content: As air conditioning becomes the subject of political debates, the European Commission is treading carefully to maintain a neutral stance.

entry.title: Keeping cool has become Europe's latest climate class war
entry.content: As Europe accelerates its green transition, the debate over air conditioning has become emblematic of a broader challenge. Climate policy is no longer judged solely by its environmental ambitions, but also by whether it can reduce emissions without deepening social inequalities.

entry.title: 'Extremely unusual' heat with 'no end' in sight, says Copernicus director
entry.content: Carlo Buontempo, Copernicus Climate Change service director, says heatwaves have become "more intense, lasting longer and starting earlier in the season" in Europe as the continent "is warming faster than the global average".

entry.title: AC should be prioritised for the elderly and vulnerable, expert says
entry.content: Diana Ürge-Vorsatz, Vice-Chair of the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change, said governments should provide "temporary measures" such as cooling centres during heatwaves. High air conditioning use is "already jeopardising" the EU's energy capacity, she said.

entry.title: Hot wax: Are your vinyls at risk during a heatwave?
entry.content: Social media users and music lovers are starting to panic about their record collections during the ongoing heatwave. Euronews Culture dispels a few myths and puts you on the right track...

entry.title: Cyprus’ tourism revenues fell by more than 35% in April as a result of the war
entry.content: Despite an initially expected downturn, Deputy Tourism Minister Kostas Koumis said tourism in Cyprus had returned to a "stable course", adding that improving performance in recent months showed government measures had "a positive impact".

entry.title: Burnham vows to 'rewire Britain' with devolution push and 'No. 10 North' in Manchester
entry.content: Labour's likely next PM set out a 10-year economic plan in his Manchester speech, promising greater regional powers and a shift away from trickle-down economics.

entry.title: The Strawberry Moon rises tonight — and no, it isn't strawberry-coloured
entry.content: The Strawberry Moon rises over Europe this evening, bringing with it centuries of tradition and absolutely no strawberry colouring whatsoever.

entry.title: ‘Frida: The Making of an Icon’: Inside Tate Modern’s highest pre-selling show
entry.content: A unibrow, flowers, bold colours — Frida Kahlo’s image is known around the world. Tate Modern’s record-breaking new exhibition brings together more than 30 works by the Mexican artist, alongside pieces by those she inspired.

entry.title: Australian charged with murder after Thai teen's body found in suitcase
entry.content: Australian man Simon Carman has been charged with murdering 17-year-old Tunchanok Donhomla after her body was found inside a suitcase in Pattaya. Police say the investigation is ongoing, while the suspect denies abducting and killing the teenager.

entry.title: At least five people killed in shooting in northern Germany, police say
entry.content: Police said they were working to establish the background to the shooting and what exactly happened.

entry.title: Ukraine's battered energy grid braces for 'intense heat' as extreme temperatures head east
entry.content: Russian drone and missile attacks have decimated Ukraine's energy network since Moscow invaded in February 2022, causing tens of billions of euros worth of damage and leading to frequent power outages.

entry.title: Taylor Swift wedding rumours: Are the upcoming nuptials this week?
entry.content: Megastar Taylor Swift is reportedly set to tie the knot with Travis Kelce at the end of this week... Or is she? Here’s everything you need to know about the rumours surrounding the nupitals.

entry.title: Stylish shopping: Prix Versailles reveals world’s most beautiful emporiums for 2026
entry.content: Seven retail locations from around the world have been chosen by Prix Versailles as the world's most beautiful emporiums in 2026.

entry.title: Aerial images reveal scale of destruction after Venezuela earthquakes
entry.content: Four days after twin quakes hit Venezuela, rescues continue at Los Cocos beach, with 1,500 dead, 189 buildings down and a father and son pulled alive.

entry.title: Thai airline employee arrested in Australia on suspicion of smuggling €302,000 of heroin
entry.content: The 26-year-old woman was on duty on board an international flight when she arrived at Melbourne Airport on Thursday.

entry.title: These are the world’s most expensive holiday destinations to visit in 2026
entry.content: From Antarctica to the Maldives, these bucket list holiday hotspots will set you back a pretty penny.

entry.title: Morocco fans light up Monterrey before Netherlands knockout clash
entry.content: Fans of Morocco and Mexico fill Monterrey’s Macroplaza with songs and celebrations ahead of Morocco’s World Cup Round of 32 clash with the Netherlands.

entry.title: Fines doubled as teens outsmart Australia's world-first social media ban
entry.content: Australia moves to double fines for social media platforms after seven in 10 children remained active on Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat and TikTok three months after the world-first ban on under-16s took effect — with Big Tech accused of "taking the Mickey."

entry.title: First Antarctic dinosaur fossil confirmed nearly 40 years after discovery
entry.content: A vertebra discovered by British scientists in 1985 has been identified as the first dinosaur fossil found in Antarctica, shedding new light on how these animals spread across the southern hemisphere.

entry.title: Starmer resigns: Which European country has the highest turnover of leaders?
entry.content: The resignation of Keir Starmer means that the UK, a European country once known for its ability to return stable governments, will see its seventh prime minister since 2016. Social media users claim this is the highest rate on the continent. Is this true?

entry.title: No Iran-US escalation boosts hopes for diplomacy, analyst says
entry.content: Osama Rizvi, founder of Rizvi Insights, said tanker traffic will continue through the Strait of Hormuz as "it serves two sides". He argued that for the US, the cost of "inaction clearly outweighs" the cost of action if tensions with Iran continue to escalate.

entry.title: Venice Commission experts to investigate Magyar's push to remove Hungary's president
entry.content: The Venice Commission will visit Hungary on Thursday to assess a constitutional amendment that would remove President Tamás Sulyok from office. Both the president and PM Péter Magyar's government say they are open to talks with the delegation.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 29th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 29th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: US marks 250th anniversary in Brussels with messages of transatlantic solidarity
entry.content: The invitation-only event drew almost 10,000 guests, including NATO chief Mark Rutte, which also sparked some local protests over the weekend-long closure of the public park.

entry.title: 'Michael' overtakes 'Oppenheimer' to become highest-grossing biopic of all time
entry.content: This year's Michael Jackson biopic also became top-grossing music biopic after surpassing 2018’s 'Bohemian Rhapsody' earlier this month.

entry.title: Heatwave: Berlin police use water cannon to cool Bruno Mars fans
entry.content: Berlin police used a water cannon to cool thousands of Bruno Mars fans queuing outside the Olympiastadion as temperatures soared during Europe's heatwave.

entry.title: Watch: Lightning hits Eiffel Tower during violent storm in Paris
entry.content: Lightning struck the Eiffel Tower as a powerful thunderstorm swept across Paris on Saturday night, sending visitors scrambling for shelter. The dramatic weather followed days of record-breaking heat in France, where authorities say the extreme temperatures contributed to a sharp rise in deaths.

entry.title: Renfe cancels 320 trains on Monday due to rail strike in Spain: Thousands of passengers hit
entry.content: The 24-hour strike is disrupting high-speed, regional and suburban rail services just as the holiday getaway is getting under way.

entry.title: One killed in shooting at California World Cup fan zone, San Jose police say
entry.content: The shooting happened at San Pedro Square, one of several places in the San Francisco Bay Area where crowds have gathered to watch World Cup matches on big outdoor screens.

entry.title: Heatwave: Which European countries are running out of water?
entry.content: As Europe endures another brutal summer, Europe in Motion unpacks water stress levels across the continent.

entry.title: Dua Lipa opens library for banned and censored books in Portugal
entry.content: The Manifesto Library is located inside Livraria Lello in Porto, and is dedicated to books “that challenge power, censorship, exclusion, and dominant narratives.”

entry.title: Can the EU stop SHEIN, Temu, and Aliexpress from dominating the e-market?
entry.content: The EU is swamped with cheap parcels imported from Chinese e-commerce giants. Products from SHEIN, Temu and the like have been benefitting from a tax loophole that will soon be axed by the EU.

entry.title: ‘Blazing Saddles’ named funniest film of all time by AFI as Mel Brooks turns 100
entry.content: Mel Brooks, who turned 100 on Sunday 28 June, has been honoured by the American Film Institute, which named his 1974 Western satire the "funniest film of all time" - knocking 'Some Like It Hot' off the top spot.

entry.title: Newsletter: Air-con culture war cranks up as heatwave moves east
entry.content: Also in this newsletter: A flavour of last night's US Indpendence bash hosted by Ambassador Bill White, and a preview of talks taking place between EU and Chinese trade chiefs in Brussels later today.

entry.title: Saunasaari: A sauna island just opened in the middle of Helsinki
entry.content: Located just a short water taxi away from the Finnish capital’s Market Square, Saunsaari is home to traditional smoke saunas and wood-fired hot tubs.

entry.title: Australia and Vanuatu sign deal barring foreign military base on Pacific island
entry.content: China formed policing ties with Vanuatu in 2023 and has donated equipment including drones, patrol boats and vehicles to its police force.

entry.title: Europe Today: Trump's tariff threat, Iran talks and Europe's heatwave
entry.content: The US and Iran are set to resume talks in Qatar after a tense weekend of renewed threats, while Europe battles a deadly heatwave linked to more than 1,300 deaths. Attention also turns to growing EU-US trade tensions and the debate over how Europe should adapt to extreme temperatures.

entry.title: Why travel has become one of the best ways to make new friends
entry.content: New research has found that almost half of Europeans believe travel is the most effective way to start new personal relationships, with shared experiences, time away from routine and a greater openness to others helping turn trips into lasting bonds.

entry.title: Brussels marks 250th anniversary of US independence with concerts, fireworks and drone show
entry.content: Thousands gathered in Brussels for the US Embassy's "Freedom 250" celebration, marking the 250th anniversary of American independence with senior Belgian, US and NATO officials in attendance.

entry.title: Why is SHEIN becoming more expensive in the EU? Ask the Euronews AI chatbot
entry.content: The EU is implementing new rules for e-commerce platforms like Temu, SHEIN, and Aliexpress. Low-value parcels will no longer be exempt from import taxes, making fast-fashion orders a lot more expensive in Europe.

entry.title: Venezuela search teams scramble as hope fades of finding earthquake survivors
entry.content: Experts say the first 72 hours after natural disasters define the narrow window for rescuing the living. After that the search usually becomes one of recovering bodies.

entry.title: Madonna, Legally Blonde and Ai Weiwei: What to see, do or hear this week in Europe
entry.content: A pinker than pink Elle Woods prequel, an Ai Weiwei exhibition, and a new Madonna album - here's what to look out for this week.

entry.title: Europe depends on China. Here's where China still depends on Europe — more than you'd think
entry.content: While Europe's dependence on China is widely recognized, China's reliance on the EU in key technologies is often overlooked. Despite its push for technological self-reliance, Beijing still depends on European capabilities in critical areas.

entry.title: Putin admits oil deficit and adjusted fuel plans amid Ukrainian strikes on refineries
entry.content: A fresh overnight Ukrainian assault set southern Russia’s major Sloviansk refinery on fire, killing one person.

entry.title: Delivery drivers, unsung heroes who kept Qatar going during Iran war
entry.content: As missiles threatened the Gulf and residents stayed indoors, delivery drivers kept delivering food and medicine across Doha, with many dubbing them heroes.

entry.title: Europe's best-value family camping destinations revealed
entry.content: New research has ranked the most affordable European countries for family camping holidays in 2026, based on campsite fees and car hire costs, with Portugal, Spain and Austria offering the best value.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 29th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 29th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Spanish film archive sets out to reconstruct Orson Welles's lifelong 'Quixote'
entry.content: The Spanish institution is joining forces with the Cinémathèque Française, Italy’s Cineteca Nazionale and Munich’s Filmmuseum to recover lost footage from a legendary shoot that unfolded over 30 years in the Spanish heartlands that so captivated the filmmaker.

entry.title: Pakistan strikes eastern Afghanistan killing dozens following Karachi attack
entry.content: Pakistan carried out airstrikes overnight in eastern Afghanistan, Islamabad said on Monday, adding its operations had targeted militants as the Afghan government reported dozens of civilian casualties.

entry.title: Iran and Oman hold first Hormuz talks as US-Iran deal stays on edge
entry.content: Iran's deputy foreign minister said the first Joint Hormuz Committee meeting took place in Muscat, after multiple US media outlets reported that fresh talks between the two sides were planned for Tuesday, a claim Tehran has since shot down, prompting Trump to counter by saying it would go ahead.

entry.title: Stars, Stripes, VIPs and Protests: Brussels hosts US Independence bash
entry.content: Among those attending are Belgian Prime Minister Bart de Wever, NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, and European Parliament President Roberta Metsola.

entry.title: Joy as man pulled from rubble three days after Venezuela quake
entry.content: Spanish and international rescue teams pulled a survivor from the rubble four days after deadly earthquakes struck Venezuela. Search operations continue as crews race to find more survivors, with the death toll rising to more than 1,400.

entry.title: Iran threatens total halt to talks amid intensive US air activity over Hormuz
entry.content: The IRGC said the weekend US strikes violated the framework deal and warned that violating vessels would face a "crushing response," as Euronews journalists in Doha observed US refuelling aircraft taking off for Hormuz in the same formation as the previous night's strikes.

entry.title: Nawrocki invites four presidents to informal summit amid dispute with Zelenskyy
entry.content: Polish President Karol Nawrocki hosted the presidents of Lithuania, Latvia, Estonia and Romania for an informal meeting in Jurata ahead of the NATO summit in Ankara.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 28th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 28th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Heavyweights Deliver a Spectacular Finish in Qingdao
entry.content: The Qingdao Grand Prix ended in style as the heavyweights took centre stage. Yahor Varapayeu claimed gold at -90kg, world No.1 Anna Monta Olek won her fourth Grand Prix title, Adam Sangariev celebrated a maiden gold, Elis Startseva dominated +78kg, and Tamerlan Bashaev returned to the top.

entry.title: Everything you need to know ahead of the World Cup knockout stage
entry.content: France have made a mightily impressive start to their campaign, scoring 10 goals in victories over Senegal, Iraq and Norway.

entry.title: Iraqi political officials arrested on corruption charges in overnight raid
entry.content: Iraqi security forces sealed off all entrances to the capital’s heavily fortified Green Zone early Sunday and carried out raids inside the compound that houses key government institutions and foreign embassies.

entry.title: Israel formally recognises Armenian World War I 'genocide'
entry.content: Israeli Foreign Minister Gideon Sa'ar said Israel had "fulfilled a moral duty by recognizing the historical truth, and rejecting attempts to deny it".

entry.title: France: 11 killed in civilian plane crash near Nancy
entry.content: Police urged the public to stay away from the area around Tomblaine airport. The aircraft was reportedly carrying skydivers.

entry.title: You can travel all around the Netherlands by train for €49 a month this summer
entry.content: This summer rail deal makes exploring the Netherlands easier and less hefty on our wallets. Here’s how to make the most of it and where else in Europe you can save on rail travel

entry.title: After 49 years of struggle, Spain leads LGBTIQ+ inclusion: inside the first Pride march
entry.content: It was 1977, two years after Franco’s death, when a group of people made history for Spain’s LGTBIQ+ community by marching on Barcelona’s Ramblas after years in the shadows.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 28th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 28th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Spain removes Gibraltar from its tax haven list after 35 years, adds Russia
entry.content: The Spanish Ministry of Finance has updated its list of non-cooperative jurisdictions, removing Gibraltar after 35 years and adding Russia, in line with the European Union's position

entry.title: Ukraine strikes two oil refineries in Russia as it continues to target Moscow's energy industry
entry.content: Ukraine's air force said local air defences had shot down six Russian ballistic missiles, one anti-ship missile and 125 drones overnight.

entry.title: Algerians celebrate as dramatic draw seals World Cup knockout spot
entry.content: "We wanted to take our revenge against Austria": Algeria fans react in Algiers after the draw against Austria secured the Fennecs' place in the World Cup round of 32.

entry.title: Hottest day: Berlin police deploy water cannons to fight extreme heat
entry.content: Germany is groaning under the year's first heatwave. Berlin police are resorting to an unusual tactic, sending two water cannons onto city streets normally reserved for protests.

entry.title: More than 68,000 people still missing in Venezuela after devastating earthquakes
entry.content: A preliminary estimate from the United Nations Development Programme put the cost of direct physical damage caused by the quakes at $6.7 billion.

entry.title: Second World War 'hell ship' found in Philippines with over 1,000 prisoners
entry.content: Divers have found the wreck of the Hofuku Maru off Luzon in the Philippines. The Japanese freighter sank in 1944 with over 1,200 Allied POWs; a digitised military file finally solved the mystery.

entry.title: World War II 'hell ship' with over 1,000 prisoners found in Philippines
entry.content: Divers have found the wreck of the Hofuku Maru off Luzon in the Philippines. The Japanese freighter sank in 1944 with over 1,200 Allied POWs; a digitised military record finally solved the mystery.

entry.title: France sees around 1,000 excess deaths during brutal heatwave
entry.content: Parts of France have topped 40°C this week, piling pressure on hospitals and first responders amid a surge in emergency callouts.

entry.title: England top World Cup group as Messi scores again for Argentina
entry.content: Croatia finished second in Group L after securing a 2-1 win over Ghana on Saturday, with goals for Nikola Vlasic and Petar Sučić.

entry.title: Where to travel in Europe and beyond, according to the world’s most travelled people
entry.content: From lesser-known UNESCO World Heritage Sites to foodie destinations you can’t miss, these are the recommendations of people who have seen it all – quite literally.

entry.title: US and Iran trade strikes amid claims of ceasefire violations
entry.content: The US also carried out strikes on Iran on Friday after a drone attack on the M/V Ever Lovely ship. US aircraft targeted Iranian missile and drone storage locations as well as coastal radar sites, CENTCOM said.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 28th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 28th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: The chicest designer beach club pop-ups in Europe this summer
entry.content: From Burberry and Dior to Jacquemus and Missoni, these are all the branded takeovers you need to know about in 2026.

entry.title: Poland's Route 666 to Hel makes its first journey
entry.content: The famous 666 bus route to the Polish town of Hel is officially back after being dropped in 2023 over concerns about its satanic associations, delighting curious passengers and tourists.

entry.title: Israel's Netanyahu welcomes US-brokered Lebanon agreement as far right condemns it
entry.content: Israel's National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir rejected the U.S.-brokered agreement, saying Lebanon could not be trusted to disarm Hezbollah..

entry.title: Serbia’s President Vučić will step down within a few weeks and calls for early elections
entry.content: “I will be president for only a couple of weeks, and then I will resign,” Vučić tells his supporters at a pro-government rally in the capital, Belgrade. Vučić’s second and last mandate expires in mid-2027.

entry.title: Europe send search and rescue personnel to Venezuela in response to massive quakes
entry.content: The European Union has begun sending aid to Venezuela through its Civil Protection Mechanism in response to Wednesday's earthquakes of 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude, which have left more than 50,000 people missing, according to the UN.

entry.title: The price of saying 'I do' in Spain: weddings cost €10,000 more in 2026
entry.content: Two recent studies put the average cost of a wedding in Spain at between 25,000 and 32,000 euros. Nearly seven in ten couples spend more than planned, and 95% admit to financial stress. Housing is the major sacrifice that follows.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 27th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 27th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Golden Score Drama and New Champions in Qingdao
entry.content: Day two of the Qingdao Grand Prix delivered world-class judo, with Narumi Tanioka winning a thrilling golden score final, Tatsuki Ishihara claiming his first Grand Prix gold, Lara Cvjetko defending her Qingdao crown, and Zelim Tckaev finishing the day in style at -81kg.

entry.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup: Which third-place teams will reach the knockout stage?
entry.content: The eight best third-place sides are determined first by points and then by goal difference, with the final standings to be decided on Saturday night.

entry.title: Rome celebrates Vespa's 80th anniversary
entry.content: Thousands of enthusiasts took to Rome’s streets to mark the Vespa’s 80th anniversary, with 160 models on show tracing the brand’s history from 1946 to today.

entry.title: Anthropic cleared to restore Mythos 5 access to certain US organisations
entry.content: "We’re restoring access for these organizations quickly, and we’re continuing to work with the government to expand access to Mythos 5 and make Fable 5 available for general use again," the company said on Friday.

entry.title: Porsche plans Cayenne return to Leipzig as staff face pay cuts
entry.content: The Cayenne is set to return to Germany. But the planned move of production to Leipzig apparently comes at a high price: staff are expected to give up part of their pay.

entry.title: German Federal Criminal Police records 486 offences against media and journalists in 2025 2025
entry.content: Left-wing offenders accounted for most bodily harm cases, while offenders linked to 'foreign ideology' accounted for the largest share of violent offences. Right-wing offenders, meanwhile, were responsible for 150 of the 486 crimes against the media.

entry.title: Do not put your phone in the fridge: 5 ways to keep your devices cool this summer
entry.content: Here are 5 ways to prevent your laptops and smartphones from getting damaged this summer.

entry.title: Woman rescued after car plunges into flooded sinkhole
entry.content: A police officer rescued a woman after her car fell into a sinkhole caused by a burst water main in Virginia, US. Bodycam footage shows bystanders helping steady the officer as he pulled the driver to safety.

entry.title: Kyiv and Moscow trade strikes as Ukraine hits arms factory deep in Russia
entry.content: Ukraine said it used its FP-5 Flamingo cruise missiles overnight to strike Russia's Titan-Barrikady weapons plant, which is thought to be involved in the production of parts for the Oreshnik missile.

entry.title: UK maritime agency raises Strait of Hormuz threat level after oil tanker reports being struck
entry.content: It comes after the US military carried out strikes on Iran, which it said were in response to Iranian drone attacks on shipping in the Strait.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 27th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 27th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Europe continues to swelter amid record-breaking temperatures
entry.content: Paris has banned drinking alcohol in public over the weekend, while the city's Pride March has also been postponed due to the heatwave.

entry.title: Venezuela earthquakes: striking satellite images before and after
entry.content: Venezuela was hit by two earthquakes last Thursday, with the La Guaira area particularly hard hit. Here is a series of before-and-after images showing the scale of the disaster.

entry.title: Venezuela satellite images reveal scale of disaster
entry.content: Venezuela was hit by a pair of earthquakes last Thursday, leaving the La Guaira area badly battered. Here is a set of before-and-after images to show the scale of the disaster.

entry.title: Searches continue for Venezuela earthquake survivors
entry.content: More than 520 emergency responders from across the EU have been mobilised to support search and rescue efforts in Venezuela, the European Commission said.

entry.title: Belgium through to World Cup knockout stage as Iran forced to wait
entry.content: Iran now face an anxious wait to see if they'll progress to the round of 32 after their final group game finished 1-1 against Egypt.

entry.title: US launches strikes against Iran following drone attack on cargo ship
entry.content: Iranian state media reported that strikes hit a telecommunications mast in the Sirik area of southern Iran.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 27th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 27th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: ‘The best scenery under heaven’: Why Guilin needs to be on your China itinerary
entry.content: Located in south west China’s Guangxi region, the area is known for its limestone karst scenery which is UNESCO World Heritage listed. In this Guilin travel guide, Dianne Apen-Sadler discovers the city’s top attractions, the cuisines to try and shares her top tips for travellers.

entry.title: Frozen feasts and cool pools help Rome zoo animals survive heatwave
entry.content: As a severe heatwave grips Italy, Rome's Bioparco is helping its animals stay cool with frozen treats, chilled pools and climate-controlled shelters while the capital remains under a red heat alert.

entry.title: Small plane crashes into Beijing's tallest building
entry.content: Police and emergency crews sealed off the area around Beijing's tallest skyscraper after a small plane crash damaged the building. Images from the scene showed a large hole in the upper floors of the 528-metre CITIC Tower, while witnesses reported seeing aircraft debris at the base of the building.

entry.title: IRGC denies direct hotline with US, as Trump warns of ceasefire violations in Strait of Hormuz
entry.content: The spokesperson for the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) denied claims by American officials regarding the establishment of a direct hotline between Iran and the United States concerning the Strait of Hormuz, calling it a "sheer lie".

entry.title: Venezuela updates earthquake toll: 920 dead, 3,360 injured
entry.content: National Assembly president Jorge Rodríguez updated the figure on Friday. The UN reports over 50,000 missing. Spain urges its citizens in Venezuela to contact consular emergency services.

entry.title: Marco Rubio announces Israel-Lebanon framework deal, hailing it as first step toward peace
entry.content: The officials did not disclose details of the deal, which was signed by Yechiel Leiter and Nada Hamadeh, Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors to the United States.

entry.title: US President Donald Trump threatens 100% tariffs if Europe implements digital services tax
entry.content: US President Donald Trump threatens to slap a 100% tariff on European countries that impose a digital services tax, adding that existing trade deals would be scrapped.

entry.title: Poor show: Ecuador win, Germany disappoint, Neuer blunders
entry.content: The German national team lost 2–1 to Ecuador in New York. Ecuador must win; Germany is already through.

entry.title: Israel declassifies Entebbe raid documents on eve of 50th anniversary
entry.content: Secret documents reveal Israeli officials privately weighed negotiations before ordering the daring 1976 Uganda hostage rescue operation.

entry.title: 'Help, we are trapped': Venezuela earthquake victims turn to Euronews' TikTok to beg for rescue"
entry.content: Lack of resources and patchy telecoms have driven many Venezuelans to despair. In this context, outlets’ social networks, including Euronews, have become key platforms for appeals for help.

entry.title: New Europol report delves into EU's most threatening criminal networks
entry.content: The EU's law enforcement agency stresses criminal networks exploit vunerabilities in financial systems and digital infrastructure, operate across borders and adapt quickly.

entry.title: ‘Very worried’: Rome airports could suspend new EES border checks over fears of summer travel chaos
entry.content: There have already been calls to put the system on hold and airports requiring extra security to manage queues.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 26th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 26th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: EU countries move to revive temporary message-scanning regime, but it could backfire
entry.content: The proposed detection regime for child sexual abuse material was rejected by the European Parliament in March, and reviving it could derail talks on a longer-term law.

entry.title: Home Heroes and Japanese Stars Shine in Qingdao
entry.content: The Qingdao Grand Prix opened with memorable performances as home favourite Wenna Zhuang delighted the local fans. Hayato Kondo claimed a second Qingdao title, Kokoro Fujishiro earned her first Grand Prix gold, Hifumi Abe added another title, Maysa Pardayeva celebrated a maiden World Tour victory.

entry.title: EU energy ministers secure greater role in bloc's future power grid after rows with Brussels
entry.content: The EU's Cypriot Presidency has successfully reached an agreement among EU countries that paves the way for talks between the European Parliament and the Council.

entry.title: Kyiv mayor at Ukraine Recovery Conference in Gdańsk: 'Ukraine must do exactly what Poland did'
entry.content: Kyiv Mayor Vitali Klitschko told the Ukraine Recovery Conference in Gdańsk that, despite recent diplomatic tensions between the two countries, the atmosphere at the gathering was friendly, and that Ukraine should look to Poland as a model for infrastructure development and modernisation.

entry.title: Russia and Ukraine swap 160 soldiers each in latest prisoner exchange
entry.content: The United Arab Emirates brokered the handover, which brought home fighters captured in the early weeks of the full-scale invasion.

entry.title: Ukraine: Zaporizhzhia ablaze after Russian missile and drone attack leaves at least nine hurt
entry.content: Russian missile and drone strikes set an office building ablaze in central Zaporizhzhia on 26 June, injuring at least nine people and triggering a major emergency response.

entry.title: To use air con or not? The facts behind the politicised battle to stay cool in Europe
entry.content: French politicians have drawn ideological battle lines over air conditioning as Europe struggles with record-breaking temperatures. What do experts say about the debate?

entry.title: Von der Leyen to visit Azerbaijan and Armenia to build on EU engagement
entry.content: The European Commission president will visit Baku and Yerevan to build on the EU’s strategic engagement with the South Caucasus and to strengthen energy cooperation, trade and regional connectivity for the EU economies.

entry.title: Watch: South Korea holds first live-fire drill against drone swarms
entry.content: South Korea's air force carried out its first live-fire exercise against a simulated swarm drone attack. Military forces used Vulcan air-defense guns, a portable laser and shotguns to intercept all 50 drones during the drill.

entry.title: Barcelona proposes to nearly triple tourist tax on cruise ship passengers to €30 a day
entry.content: The hiked fee would only apply to cruises that make port calls and spend less than 12 hours docked.

entry.title: Can travel solve the loneliness epidemic? WeRoad's co-founder Fabio Bin thinks so
entry.content: Speaking to Euronews in Cannes after being shortlisted for the European CMO of the Year Award, Fabio Bin explained he turned a personal frustration into a fast-growing social travel brand.

entry.title: Buurtzorg: the Dutch proximity health care revolution
entry.content: In the Netherlands, Buurtzorg’s self-managed nurses provide holistic home care and social support, helping older people live independently with dignity.

entry.title: 'Marketing is more important than ever': Serviceplan CEO at European CMO of the Year awards
entry.content: Speaking to Euronews, Serviceplan Group CEO Florian Haller said marketing has evolved far beyond traditional advertising, and now sits at the centre of the "whole customer journey" where "data, technology and creativity" meet.

entry.title: Lebanon: thousands mark Ashoura in Nabatieh amid rubble left by Israeli strikes
entry.content: Thousands of Shiite Muslims marked Ashoura in Nabatieh on 26 June, holding processions through neighbourhoods devastated by recent Israeli airstrikes in a powerful display of resilience.

entry.title: Oil, cod, Kirill: friction points emerge in new EU sanctions against Russia
entry.content: The EU wants to agree on a new round of measures targeting Moscow before 15 July, but the closed-door negotiations have difficult obstacles to navigate.

entry.title: Europe's best lakes for a safe summer dip, from the Italian Alps to the Hungarian plains
entry.content: The report ranked European countries for their abundance of lakes rated as “excellent”, meaning their water quality was safe for swimming.

entry.title: Fact check: Were you fooled by these AI-generated images of the World Cup?
entry.content: Watching tense, fast-paced football games requires focus, but so does consuming World Cup content online, because false images surrounding the tournament are becoming increasingly common.

entry.title: 'Measures needed to face extreme heat or it will kill us,' Copernicus director says
entry.content: As scorching weather hits Europe, climate expert Carlo Buontempo told Euronews' flagship morning show Europe Today that "record-breaking temperatures should not be breaking news" and that strategies are needed to face extreme weather lest humanity suffers the consequences.

entry.title: Life sentence for Magdeburg attacker
entry.content: When he drove a car into the Magdeburg Christmas market, he killed six people and injured more than 300. Taleb al Abdulmohsen has now been sentenced to life imprisonment.

entry.title: Greece to send astronaut into space: Adrianos Golemis to launch within two years
entry.content: The Greek astronaut will conduct experiments and technology demonstrations proposed by dozens of Greek research teams, institutions and universities.

entry.title: Why is there so much outrage over FIFA’s ‘hydration breaks’ at this year's World Cup?
entry.content: Every World Cup game has been interrupted due to new scheduled "hydration breaks", a move from FIFA which has been met with a chorus of boos. What’s behind the interruptions, and why are they so controversial?

entry.title: EU investigators seize 4,200 tonnes of textile waste illegally shipped to Turkey from Italy
entry.content: Inspectors discovered an additional 2,100 tonnes of textile waste stored in a warehouse linked to a Turkish recycling facility that was noncompliant with environmental laws. Meanwhile, France is urging EU countries to get tough on ultra-fast fashion.

entry.title: Ukraine launches 40-day operation to ‘pressure Russia to end the war’
entry.content: After several weeks of a strike campaign against energy and military targets in Moscow-occupied Crimea, Ukraine is now launching a 40-day operation aimed at “pressuring Russia to end its war”.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 26th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 26th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Hospitals struggle as European heatwave moves east
entry.content: As hospitals buckle and warnings spread east, the heatwave is exposing how unprepared much of Europe remains for extreme temperatures.

entry.title: Why Erling Haaland has become the World Cup’s most entertaining star
entry.content: Whether he's posing as Norway's 'social media guy' or sharing candid shots in an infrared mask, Erling Haaland is winning over fans around the world.

entry.title: Brussels to exclude military-age Ukrainian men from temporary protection scheme
entry.content: Despite Ukraine's imposition of compulsory military service, some draft-age Ukrainian men have crossed the border illegally and subsequently received temporary protection in EU countries.

entry.title: Montenegro police arrest Iranian accused of hacking US universities
entry.content: The suspect is accused of helping steal data from more than 150 US universities over a hacking campaign dating back to 2013.

entry.title: Iraq: Ashoura in Karbala draws thousands of Shiite pilgrims for rituals
entry.content: Thousands of Shiite pilgrims mark Ashoura in Karbala, mourning Imam Hussein’s 680 AD death with processions, prayers and reinforced security.

entry.title: EU ban on Russian combatants faces resistance from France and Italy
entry.content: Rome and Paris have cited legal and technical concerns over how the ban should be applied in practice.

entry.title: Venezuela earthquakes: bodies recovered as rescue efforts continue in La Guaira
entry.content: Rescue teams worked through the ruins of collapsed buildings in La Guaira on 25 June after twin earthquakes devastated northern Venezuela, killing at least 188 people and injuring around 1,500.

entry.title: Podcast | From Nicosia to Dublin: Cyprus to pass the baton to Ireland
entry.content: From the long-term EU budget to the conflict in the Middle East, what were the defining moments of Cyprus' presidency of the Council of the EU? And as Ireland takes over, what fresh ideas will it bring to the table?

entry.title: Peppa Pig backlash as US company Hasbro requires child actors to sign voices over to AI
entry.content: US entertainment giant Hasbro is reportedly asking child actors on the popular series Peppa Pig to sign over their voices to artificial intelligence under new contract terms. An open letter signed by 1,000 industry professionals condemns the AI contract clause and warns of matters of consent.

entry.title: Amid shifts in energy supply, which EU countries consume and produce the most gas?
entry.content: Natural gas demand is climbing across the EU, even as domestic production struggles to keep pace. How do member states compare?

entry.title: Tehran will allow nuclear inspections during peace talks, former Iranian diplomat says
entry.content: Seyed Hossein Mousavian, former spokesman for Iran's nuclear negotiation team, tells Euronews he believes Tehran will allow the IAEA access to its nuclear sites as part of negotiations with the US, despite some Iranian officials saying access could only be granted after a final deal has been struck.

entry.title: Turkey produces last minute winner to beat the US as Germany end win streak with loss to Ecuador
entry.content: The final matchday saw teams take on both more cautious and reckless approaches as they looked to secure qualification into the knockout stages.

entry.title: UK foreign policy to remain unchanged despite government transition, deputy PM Lammy says
entry.content: The UK's deputy Prime Minister David Lammy has told Europe Today that his country's foreign policy — including its support for Ukraine and efforts to restore EU ties — will remain unchanged when a new Prime Minister takes the reins after Keir Starmer's exit.

entry.title: Historic joy in Abidjan as Ivory Coast reach World Cup knockouts for first time
entry.content: Ivory Coast reaches World Cup knockout stage for the first time after Nicolas Pépé’s brace beats Curaçao 2-0, sparking huge celebrations in Abidjan.

entry.title: Newsletter: Temperatures rise over sanctions against Russia
entry.content: In this newsletter: Bulgaria attempts to bulldoze part of the EU's 21st sanctions package against Russia, criticism mounts over the EU's hyped rule of law reports, and a baby makes an appearance at a council meeting in Luxembourg.

entry.title: Japan fans flood Shibuya as World Cup draw with Sweden sets up Brazil clash
entry.content: Japan reach World Cup last 32 after 1-1 draw with Sweden on 26 June, prompting celebrations across Tokyo. , sparking huge celebrations in Tokyo’s Shibuya. They now face Brazil in the knockout stage.

entry.title: Harvey Weinstein's New York rape charge dropped - accuser says she can't endure a fourth trial
entry.content: Weinstein still stands convicted of another sexual felony in New York and others in California. He remains behind bars at New York's maximum-security prison, Rikers Island.

entry.title: EU releases €3 billion loan for Ukraine as US-Iran talks remain fragile
entry.content: Ukraine has received 3.2 billion euros as the first disbursement under the European Union's 90 billion euro support loan. Meanwhile, Iranians are rejecting claims that the UN would visit nuclear enrichment sites in the country.

entry.title: How Portugal's Alentejo region has become a hotspot for luxury hotel openings
entry.content: New openings, villa relaunches and stylish boutique stays are drawing fresh attention to Portugal’s Alentejo region, from Comporta and Melides to the historic streets of Évora.

entry.title: Euronews Culture's Film of the Week: 'Supergirl' - Save the puppy, save the world
entry.content: After a strong start to the rebooted DC Universe last year with 'Superman' comes a new space adventure which follows the Man of Steel's chaotic cousin... And her super-powered pup.

entry.title: Gulf states weigh living side by side with Iran as Tehran weaponises Hormuz
entry.content: As the frontline countries paying the largest price for the Iran war, pressed by severe economic urgencies, the Gulf states are beginning their own firm process towards regional stability with rival Iran, while Tehran continues weaponising the Strait of Hormuz to sow division with its neighbours.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 26th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 26th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Côte d’Ivoire: Discover West Africa’s beach and culture hotspot
entry.content: Regarded as West Africa’s answer to the Caribbean, Côte d’Ivoire has an extraordinary coast, UNESCO-listed culture and direct flights from Europe.

entry.title: Europe’s Taliban Dilemma: the struggle to return Afghan migrants
entry.content: EU countries are engaging with Afghanistan’s Taliban-led regime to return irregular migrants deemed security threats or convicted of serious offences. Yet any such arrangement could carry political and diplomatic costs, requiring concessions and risking public backlash.

entry.title: Death toll from Venezuela earthquakes rises to 235, search and rescue efforts continue
entry.content: The two quakes struck in quick succession, just minutes apart, according to the US Geological Survey, marking it one of the South American nation’s worst disasters in over a century.

entry.title: US Supreme Court clears way for mass deportations of Haitians and Syrians
entry.content: The 6-3 ruling hands Donald Trump a sweeping victory on immigration, stripping legal protections from hundreds of thousands of people the State Department warns would be returning to dangerous countries.

entry.title: EU's door 'is open to UK', fomer Brexit negotiator Barnier tells Euronews
entry.content: Ten years following the landmark vote, the EU's former Brexit negotiator said the bloc's door is open to the UK, but that London "cannot cherry-pick" EU policies.

entry.title: Football for friends: Winning ways World Cup supporters have gone viral – for the right reasons
entry.content: Food discoveries, tidy stadiums and bagpipes on the streets of Boston are only the beginning as football fans across the United States, Canada and Mexico have been leaving very distinctive signs of their presence...

entry.title: Cargo ship hit by projectile in Hormuz as Iran issues renewed passage warning
entry.content: The British military said a cargo ship on the new Omani UN-backed route sustained bridge damage from a projectile, hours after the IRGC warned that vessels without Iranian permission were transiting "illegally".
